mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and contraction in consumer price inflation indicates robust demand growth without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during recovery phases or 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 50% during payroll expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent signals weakening construction demand and reduced household wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compresses real interest rates and signals delayed monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect yields positive returns in greater than 55% of cases or real rate compression fails to predict equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter expansion in real gross domestic product confirms broad-based economic growth and supports corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered instances during early-cycle phases or loses significance in walk-forward testing.
